title: Rules at the Museum
learningGoal: The learner will read a set of public guidelines to distinguish between actions that are permitted (~te mo ii desu) and actions that are strictly prohibited (~te wa ikemasen).
instructions: Read the following informational sign posted inside a Japanese art museum. Identify which activities are allowed and which are banned.

contentItems.text: Pay attention to the ending of each sentence. 'ikemasen' means absolutely not. 'ii desu' means it is fine.

readingText: ここは びじゅつかんです。あぶないですから、はしってはいけません。そして、ここで しゃしんを とってはいけません。たばこを すってはいけません。でも、けいたいでんわを つかっても いいです。ここに すわっても いいです。
textInTargetLanguage: ここは びじゅつかんです。あぶないですから、はしってはいけません。そして、ここで しゃしんを とってはいけません。たばこを すってはいけません。でも、けいたいでんわを つかっても いいです。ここに すわっても いいです。
baseLanguageSupportText: This is an art museum. Because it is dangerous, you must not run. And, you must not take photos here. You must not smoke tobacco. But, you may use a mobile phone. You may sit here.

glossary.targetText: はしってはいけません
つかっても いいです
すわっても いいです

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: あぶないですから、はしってはいけません。
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Abunai desu kara, hashitte wa ikemasen.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: Because it is dangerous, you must not run.

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: ここで しゃしんを とってはいけません。
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Koko de shashin wo totte wa ikemasen.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: You must not take photos here.

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: けいたいでんわを つかっても いいです。
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Keitai denwa wo tsukatte mo ii desu.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: You may use a mobile phone.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Which of the following actions IS permitted?
Write the romaji for the verb 'to sit' in its dictionary (masu) form.

expectedSummaryPoints: The rules are for an art museum.
Running, smoking, and taking photos are banned.
Using a phone and sitting are allowed.

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! You clearly understand the difference between permission and prohibition.
feedbackRules.onFail: Look at the end of the verb. If it ends in 'ikemasen', you cannot do it. If it ends in 'ii desu', you can do it.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Review the first sentence to recognize the word for Museum (Bijutsukan).

audioAssets.audioTranscript: ここは びじゅつかんです。あぶないですから、はしってはいけません。そして、ここで しゃしんを とってはいけません。たばこを すってはいけません。でも、けいたいでんわを つかっても いいです。ここに すわっても いいです。

hintText: Pay attention to 'kara' in 'abunai desu kara'. It means 'because'.
commonMistakes: Thinking 'Keitai denwa' is prohibited because it is grouped with other things, but its sentence ends in 'ii desu'.
